layman_headline: Crypto markets are in deep fear territory — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin has dropped below $60,000 and the overall crypto market mood is at 'Extreme Fear' — one of the lowest readings we've seen. Almost every altcoin is bleeding, with many down 15-25% in the past week. While extreme fear can eventually create great buying opportunities, the selling hasn't shown clear signs of stopping yet. Trying to catch a falling knife in this environment is risky.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is still falling and there's no clear bottom signal yet. Keep watching for stabilization — a day or two where prices stop dropping and volume picks up on the buy side would be the green light.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate has significant technical problems. VELVET is up 269% in a week with RSI at 70 — textbook overbought after a parabolic move, terrible risk/reward for entry. KITE and ENA have strongly bearish chart structures with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu patterns. WLD dropped 25% with a slightly bearish chart bias and only 1x risk/reward. SOL is the relative outperformer but remains below all major moving averages with a bearish chart bias of -0.70 and a Doji candle showing indecision. The Fear & Greed index at 18 with a worsening trend suggests capitulation is underway but not yet complete — BTC UTXO data confirms this per CoinTelegraph. BTC dominance at 55.9% means capital is fleeing alts to BTC or stables. The macro news flow is uniformly negative. The disciplined play is to wait for RSI to hit true oversold (<30) on a quality asset, or for a clear reversal pattern to form.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $58K support, triggering cascading liquidations and another 10-15% altcoin drawdown
Strategy potentially selling $3B in BTC and Binance's $400M+ outflows could accelerate selling pressure
High BTC dominance trend and worsening Fear & Greed suggest alt season is nowhere close — alts could underperform for weeks
watch_list: BTC price action around $58,000-$60,000 — a decisive break below $58K would signal deeper correction; a bounce with volume would signal opportunity
Fear & Greed index — watch for a reversal from 'worsening' trend to 'improving'; historically extreme readings below 15 mark bottoms
SOL holding $66 support — if it holds and RSI drops below 35, it becomes a high-conviction mean-reversion entry
market_context: The crypto market is in a significant correction with BTC below $60K and tracking a rare back-to-back quarterly loss. Extreme Fear at 18/100 with a worsening trend, negative macro headlines, and institutional outflows suggest the bottom is not yet confirmed.
context_banner: Market capitulation is underway with Bitcoin below $60K and Extreme Fear at 18 — discipline means waiting for the reversal signal, not guessing the bottom.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 18 with a worsening weekly trend (avg 16.9) signals active capitulation — historically a precondition for bottoms but timing is uncertain.
Bitcoin UTXO analysis signals capitulation is underway, confirming on-chain stress aligns with sentiment readings.
Grayscale's Pandl suggesting Strategy sell $3B in BTC to 'restore confidence' introduces significant potential sell pressure overhang.
Gold and silver selloff dragging Bitcoin down indicates this is a broader risk-off move across all hard assets, not crypto-specific weakness.
No candidate has RSI below 35 — SOL and KITE at ~41 are approaching oversold but haven't reached levels that reliably mark swing bottoms.
